title: Psychotria eciliata
taxonomy: Psychotria eciliata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which comprises over 600 genera of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family is part of the order Gentianales. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. Psychotria eciliata is a tropical plant species native to the Americas.

intro: Psychotria elatior, also known as Psychotria elatior, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria elatior include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as elatine and elatine methyl ester, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
